canonical_nt.summary: Named NT defect tranche is complete and guarded.

canonical_ot.summary: Canonical OT has source-backed NABRE overlay remediation, residual classification, Nehemiah 7:73 adapter policy, and Daniel 11 source-absent governed exclusion recorded. It remains pending final canonical OT trust audit.

psalms.summary: Psalms are a governed 155-record legacy psalter subsystem and remain not textually trusted.

deuterocanon.summary: Greek Esther and Greek Daniel are governed in active NRSV form without text mutation. 3/4 Maccabees source acquisition is closed for active NRSV lane. 4 Maccabees 10:4 and 11:7-8 are governed variant-footnote material, not active primary rows.

broader_canon.summary: Broader canon raw-text corpus is governed but not source-adjudicated or textually trusted.

vulgate.summary: Vulgate pilot lanes are committed as bounded source-lane data. Vulgate build, expansion, source acquisition, and integration are deferred until the active non-Vulgate corpus is trusted; existing Vulgate pilot work may be audited later.

latestCanonicalOTDaniel11Policy.summary: The preserved controlled NABRE source lane at commit d0eed049 contains Daniel chapter 11 with an empty verses array. Daniel 11:1-45 remains without NABRE active text by governed exclusion. Do not synthesize NABRE, scrape substitute text, or overlay from another translation. Canonical OT may proceed only to final trust audit with this exclusion recorded.

vulgateDeferralPolicy.summary: Do not build, expand, source-acquire, or integrate Vulgate until the active non-Vulgate corpus is trusted. After active non-Vulgate trust is established, audit the existing Vulgate pilot state before deciding whether any further Vulgate buildout is authorized.

latestCanonicalOTNehemiah773AdapterPolic.summary: Nehemiah 7:73 is not a missing-row defect. NRSV/Rotherham/KJV active row 7:73 contains both the settlement close and the seventh-month transition. NABRE places the settlement close at 7:72 and the seventh-month transition at 8:1. Preserve source text by source address; any harmonized display requires adapter/view policy rather than synthetic row construction.

latestGreekEstherFormPolicy.summary: Active Greek Esther is governed as NRSV named-Addition form plus ordinary Esther chapters. DRB/NABRE row-grid deltas are not repair instructions. Do not rewrite to DRB numbered extension chapters, NABRE row grid, or ordinary Esther-only form without a separate canonical-form migration policy.

latestDeuterocanonSourceShapeReview.summary: Greek Esther and Greek Daniel source-shape review found no safe simple overlay. Greek Esther has substantial active/DRB/ordinary Esther form divergence. Greek Daniel is closer to DRB but remaining mismatches fall in Greek-addition territory. No Bible text was changed.

latestMaccabees34SourceState.summary: Current active 3 and 4 Maccabees files already carry NRSV source-backed repair metadata from BibleGateway NRSVA and the historical repair commit 8177b52. Raw source acquisition is no longer the next blocker for these files; further work is bounded source/form review.

latestMaccabees4VariantFootnotePolicy.summary: BibleGateway NRSVA presents 4 Maccabees 10:4 and 11:7-8 as other-authority footnote additions, not primary body-text rows. They remain governed variant material and must not be inserted into active rows without a separate variant apparatus policy.

latestGreekDanielFormPolicy.summary: Active Greek Daniel is governed as one NRSV extended Daniel book: Daniel 1-12, Prayer of Azariah/Song embedded in chapter 3, Susanna as chapter 13, and Bel and the Dragon as chapter 14. DRB/NABRE row-grid deltas are not repair instructions.
